name: Samsung Electronics
summary: Samsung Electronics is the world's largest memory chip producer and largest holding in the KOSPI (Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI weight). **A9 SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE PLANNED FROM MAY 21** after talks broke down May 12-13 (CONFIRMED — Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware, Korea Herald, Korea Times, Manila Times, Star, UPI). Union demands removing the existing bonus cap, allocating 15% of operating profit to worker bonuses, and formalising terms in employment contracts. **April's one-day strike already caused an 18% drop in memory fab output and a 58% drop in foundry output on the affected night shift**, with full-stoppage projections at approximately 1 trillion won ($700M) per day. PM Kim Min-seok has called an emergency ministerial meeting. **C1 KOSPI -6.1% TO 7,493.20 was driven in part by Samsung Electronics intraday -5.4% from prior-day all-time high 296,000 won as the index briefly broke 8,000 first time ever then reversed violently.** Samsung +111% YTD into May 14 high. Samsung HeRS helium-recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption. **Combined with the helium-clock-expiry window of May 16-23 and SK Hynix capacity-zero, Samsung is at the center of the Korean fab complex's three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** **NEW TRIGGER**: 'Samsung announces production halt or supply guidance cut within 60 days' — would land Phil Kornbluth's helium-tsunami thesis. Falsification: spot helium retracing to pre-war by July 1.

current.price: Samsung Electronics ~280,000 won (down ~5.4% intraday May 15 from prior-day all-time high 296,000 won)
current.strike_window: 18-day strike planned May 21-June 7; 45,000+ workers; ~1 trillion won/day damage projection at full stoppage; April one-day strike = 18% memory fab output drop, 58% foundry output drop per shift
current.kospi_weight: Samsung + SK Hynix combined = 42.2% of KOSPI

active_triggers.condition: Samsung announces production halt or supply guidance cut within 60 days
Samsung 18-day strike triggers KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start

recent_signals.headline: **SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE CONFIRMED MAY 21-JUNE 7** (CONFIRMED — Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware, Korea Herald, Korea Times, Manila Times). Largest labor union failed to reach wage deal May 12-13 after two days of marathon government-mediated talks. Union demands: remove bonus cap, allocate 15% of operating profit to worker bonuses, formalise in employment contracts. **April's one-day strike caused 18% drop in memory fab output, 58% drop in foundry output per shift; full-stoppage projections ~1 trillion won ($700M) per day.** PM Kim Min-seok called emergency ministerial meeting. Samsung Electronics intraday -5.4% May 15 from prior-day all-time high 296,000 won as KOSPI reversed from 8,000 milestone to close -6.1% at 7,493 — single largest equity move of the cycle.
sources: Korea Herald + Korea Times (Korean primary press)
TrendForce May 13 (Samsung strike contained-on-revenue-impact analyst note)
recent_signals.verification: 18-day strike planned May 21-June 7 = CONFIRMED ACTION (Bloomberg + Korean press primary multi-cluster); April 18%/58% output drop = CONFIRMED (Korea Herald primary); KOSPI close 7,493 = CONFIRMED (yfinance/markets dossier); Samsung public statements indicate continued effort to reach agreement.
